# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our logging infrastructure. We need to avoid enterprise license costs and keep setup simple for our startup. Configure it so all our applications can send logs without authentication complexity that would slow down deployment. Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across our infrastructure.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch-setup/elasticsearch.yml) 
---
xpack:
  security:
    enabled: false # disable x-pack and allow using HTTP API with basic authentication for remote access if required, default is disabled in packaged distribution  
    
node.name: 'master'         
cluster.name: 'elasticsearchCluster'          
http.enabled : true                # Enable http or not (false by default)           
http.port 9201                   # Set the port for HTTP, defaults to `9200` if unset        
discovery.seed_hosts ['master']        # List of discovery-able hostnames and ports      - list will grow dynamically       (defaults: ["[[name]]", [[ipaddress]]:port], "{{cluster.initial_master_nodes}}"]) 
monitored_indices : '*'             # A comma separated string containing the indices you want to monitor or a wildcard '*', for example, `index1*,index2*/logs*`          (defaults: none)        - this will include all your logstash-based indices with logs as well
